Flow map: Arrival -> registration -> triage RN applies ESI decision A/B/C/D -> waiting/treatment area -> provider assessment -> labs/imaging/consults -> disposition
Roles involved: patient, registration, triage RN, ED clinician, lab/radiology
Key data collected: chief complaint, high-risk signs, vitals, resource needs
Decision points: life-saving intervention? high risk? how many resources? vitals danger zone?
Black zones embedded: triage downgrade if complex but stable; pre-triage queue; prior visits not visible

Flow map: Arrival -> register -> initial assessment: streaming/triage/RAT -> ED, UTC, SDEC/specialty, or off-site service -> ongoing treatment/discharge
Roles involved: patient, registration, registered clinician, RAT team, ED/UTC/specialty
Tools/forms/scores: streaming, triage, RAT, early warning scores
Key data collected: chief complaint, acuity, disposition, vitals
Decision points: sickest first? right service? immediate treatment plan?
Black zones embedded: occult waits before registration; unsafe redirection; poor handoff between services

Flow map: Walk-in/EMS -> front desk/triage RN -> intake RN -> provider evaluation -> labwork + chest X-ray -> CAP? -> CURB-65 -> admit vs outpatient antibiotics vs pursue other diagnosis
Roles involved: front desk, triage RN, intake RN, provider, lab/radiology
Key data collected: age, RR, BP, confusion, BUN, CXR
Decision points: CAP likely? severity? inpatient warranted?
Black zones embedded: anchoring on CAP; missing alternative diagnoses; missing data elements

Flow map: Walk-in/booked/111 -> arrival -> nurse observations -> assessed by UTC clinician -> tests if available -> treated/prescribed or referred/escalated -> GP update
Roles involved: reception, nurse, UTC clinician, GP/ED/specialist
Key data collected: symptoms, observations, tests
Decision points: treat onsite? need ED/specialist? has patient worsened?
Black zones embedded: patients wait by acuity; deterioration relies on patient telling staff; diagnostics vary

Flow map: Arrival -> patient self-check-in on tablet/kiosk -> demographic match/NHS number -> guided symptom triage -> prioritise/redirect/refer -> clinician receives triage outcome
Roles involved: patient, digital system, streaming clinician, receiving service
Key data collected: demographics, symptoms, triage outcome
Decision points: stay in ED/UTC? pharmacy/other service? urgent priority?
Black zones embedded: digital access/language; symptom nuance; no human observation during form completion

Flow map: Before visit -> patient enters age/sex/postcode + main symptom -> NHS Pathways questions -> outcome: 999/ED/UTC/GP/pharmacist/nurse callback/self-care -> receiving service
Roles involved: patient, algorithm, callback clinician, Directory of Services
Key data collected: main symptom, structured answers, location
Decision points: emergency? callback? local service?
Black zones embedded: one-symptom-at-a-time; complex known conditions; directory mismatch

Flow map: Patient contacts by phone/online/in-person -> care navigation/admin or clinical triage -> GP/nurse/pharmacist/other professional -> labs/imaging/referral or self-care -> safety-net/follow-up
Roles involved: patient, receptionist/care navigator, nurse/GP/pharmacist
Tools/forms/scores: care navigation, clinical triage, online consultation
Key data collected: reason, urgency, history, risk flags
Decision points: admin request or clinical? same day? right professional?
Black zones embedded: routing without enough clinical detail; repeated contacts not linked

Flow map: Clinician orders test -> patient attends or not -> result returns or not -> trained staff/clinician review -> patient informed -> abnormal follow-up/referral -> filing/closure
Roles involved: GP, nurse, admin, lab/imaging provider, patient
Key data collected: order, attendance, result, abnormality, reviewer, action
Decision points: result returned? abnormal? who informs patient? action completed?
Black zones embedded: lost/non-returned results; clinician absence; filing without action

Flow map: Initial visit -> low-risk/negative tests -> safety-net plan -> repeat presentation -> third presentation triggers reflect/review/rethink -> exam/peer review/tests/referral -> diagnosis or monitored plan
Roles involved: patient, GP team, peer reviewer, specialist
Tools/forms/scores: Cancer Research UK safety netting, Jess's Rule
Key data collected: symptom duration, repeat count, tests, referrals, red flags
Decision points: persistent? escalating? unexplained? third visit?
Black zones embedded: normal-test trap; multiple clinicians; repeat count across channels missing

Flow map: GP/referrer creates e-RS referral -> provider RAS triages before booking -> book/redirect/return advice/request more info -> patient appointment or primary-care management -> follow-up
Roles involved: referrer, provider triage clinician/admin, patient
Key data collected: referral info, prior tests, triage comments
Decision points: sufficient info? right service? appointment needed?
Black zones embedded: wrong worklist/status; patient confusion; advice without safety net

Flow map: GP symptom concern -> specialist advice/guidance or straight-to-test -> diagnostic test/consultant review -> advice, appointment or treatment pathway -> safety-net
Roles involved: GP, specialist consultant, diagnostic service
Key data collected: referral criteria, symptoms, prior tests, diagnostic question
Decision points: can avoid first appointment? needs endoscopy/imaging?
Black zones embedded: rejected referral; advice-only drift; missing critical context

Flow map: Population empanelment -> ASHA/home contact -> HWC screening age 30+ -> CHO/doctor assessment -> referral for suspected NCD/cancer/mental health -> two-way follow-up
Roles involved: ASHA, ANM/MPW, CHO, doctor, referral facility
Tools/forms/scores: HWC registry, screening forms, referral cards
Key data collected: household list, BP/glucose/cancer screen, mental health questionnaire
Decision points: positive screen? needs referral? follow-up compliance?
Black zones embedded: positive screens not tracked; referral loop not closed; low-resource constraints

Flow map: OPD arrival -> nurse applies IITT-adapted checklist/color category on chart -> emergency/urgent/non-urgent prioritisation -> clinician assessment -> treatment/referral/discharge
Roles involved: OPD nurse, clinician, patient
Key data collected: presenting complaint, vitals, danger signs
Decision points: emergency/urgent? wait priority?
Black zones embedded: undertriage; staff training; paper chart data not aggregated

Flow map: Caregiver/CHW contact -> CHW assesses fever/cough/diarrhoea and danger signs -> classifies/treats uncomplicated illness -> refers severe cases -> supervisor/facility follow-up
Roles involved: CHW, caregiver, supervisor, referral facility
Key data collected: symptoms, danger signs, treatment stock, referral advice
Decision points: uncomplicated vs severe? has medicine? can refer?
Black zones embedded: stockout; referral transport; lack of counter-referral

Flow map: Geographic assignment -> CHW home visits/registers family -> notes symptoms/living conditions/adherence -> resolves low-level issue or relays/refers to nurse/physician -> primary-care follow-up
Roles involved: CHW, nurse, physician, dental team, family
Key data collected: living conditions, symptoms, medication adherence, social needs
Decision points: can CHW resolve? refer to nurse/physician? chronic follow-up needed?
Black zones embedded: community signals not structured; no closed loop to clinician

Flow map: Eligibility/GP register -> FIT kit mailed -> patient collects sample -> lab result -> no blood routine recall OR further tests -> specialist nurse -> usually colonoscopy -> result/follow-up
Roles involved: screening programme, patient, lab, specialist nurse, colonoscopy team, GP
Key data collected: sample, result category, symptoms, colonoscopy outcome
Decision points: blood detected? further tests? symptoms despite negative?
Black zones embedded: kit non-return; positive FIT no colonoscopy; false reassurance after negative

Flow map: Insurer eligibility age 40+ -> annual checkup/questionnaire/labs -> risk stratification -> information/motivational/proactive support -> 3+ month evaluation
Roles involved: insurer, physician, public health nurse, nutritionist
Key data collected: questionnaire, abdominal girth, BP, lipids, liver/metabolic labs, urinalysis
Decision points: risk stratum? needs guidance? evaluation complete?
Black zones embedded: nonattendance; support drop-off; risk not connected to primary care

Flow map: NHIS eligibility notice -> patient schedules/visits authorized hospital with ID/card -> screening -> results mailed -> confirmation checkup if suspected disease
Roles involved: NHIS, authorized hospital/clinic, patient
Key data collected: eligibility, fasting, test results, suspected disease flag
Decision points: needs confirmation? result understood?
Black zones embedded: mail result not understood; confirmation follow-up gap

Flow map: GP sees symptoms -> alarm symptoms to organ-specific CPP OR serious nonspecific symptoms -> initial tests/blood panel -> diagnostic centre -> cancer/serious disease work-up
Roles involved: GP, diagnostic centre, hospital specialists
Tools/forms/scores: CPP/NSSC pathway, GP gut feeling, blood panel
Key data collected: weight loss, fatigue, abnormal bloods, fever, pain, GP gut feeling
Decision points: alarm vs nonspecific? tests inconclusive? diagnostic centre?
Black zones embedded: patients below criteria; vague symptoms managed locally without robust safety net

Flow map: Family doctor/HMO clinician referral -> collection/imaging site intake -> test performed -> result disseminated to physician computer/user station; teleradiology if distant -> clinician/patient follow-up
Roles involved: family doctor, collection site staff, lab/radiology specialist, patient
Tools/forms/scores: HMO EMR, computerized lab and teleradiology systems
Key data collected: referral, sample/image, result, mammography follow-up program
Decision points: result normal/abnormal? action documented?
Black zones embedded: result availability without action; alert fatigue; patient notification gap
